branches: Special Operations Forces (Aitvaras) - size not publicly disclosed
equipment: M142 HIMARS (8 systems, additional battery on order)

procurement.item: Mercedes-Benz G-Class, Unimog, Zetros and Arocs vehicles
Additional HIMARS battery (launchers, munitions, C2 and logistics package)
procurement.delivery: Following 2022 order; full operational capability expected 2027
Direct award, citing essential security interests

news: Lithuania signed a USD 280 million supplemental agreement with the US for an additional HIMARS battery, including launchers, guided munitions, C2 equipment and a maintenance package (2026-05-05).
The State Defence Council approved procurement of PATRIA 6x6 wheeled armoured personnel carriers to support the new National Division, targeted for 2030 (2026-05-27).
Lithuania's Cabinet authorised a direct-award purchase of 48 Merops AS-3 interceptor drones from Perennial Autonomy, citing essential security interests (2026-04-22).

The Lithuanian Cabinet approved a Defence Ministry proposal to simplify and speed up military procurement, applying streamlined industrial-cooperation mechanisms to strategically important contracts (2026-06-03).
Lithuanian firm builds GPS-free tool for drone strikes (2026-06-18).
Lietuvos futbolo čempionato turo anonsas: sudėtingos išvykos žalgiriečiams (2026-06-20).
Atrankos langą pasitinkantis Kurtinaitis – apie rinktinės talentą ir Buzelio poziciją (2026-06-22).
Lietuvos lengvaatlečiai Italijoje pasiekė du geriausius sezono rezultatus (2026-06-24).

upcoming_major_purchase.item: Mercedes-Benz G-Class / Unimog / Zetros / Arocs military vehicle fleet
upcoming_major_purchase.quantity: Multiple vehicle types across several thousand units (exact unit breakdown not yet published)
upcoming_major_purchase.notes: Described by officials as one of the largest investments in Lithuanian military logistics history; contract already signed, deliveries beginning 2026.

nato_presence.notes: Brigade-level presence being built up; troop numbers fluctuate with rotations.
